mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is trending down over the prior quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods when FEDFUNDS is declining over a 3-month window, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 3-year span.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and lower future demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after a 0.3pp/3m unemployment rise is not statistically below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS together with rising HOUST implies resilient consumer demand and construction activity, supporting near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not outperform either series alone, or if high-RSAFS/high-HOUST states fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns relative to the unconditional sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in INDPRO indicates stronger factory output and inventory replenishment, which tends to lift cyclically sensitive earnings and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with INDPRO rising more than 1.0% over 2 months do not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real rates are negative and policy is accommodative, which supports equity valuation multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI minus FEDFUNDS spread fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns are not concentrated in periods where nominal policy is below inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 relative to recent quarters signals weakening aggregate demand and lower earnings revisions, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP growth below 1.0% does not precede below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is positive across any 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating from both employment and unemployment angles, which weakens consumption prospects and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth turns negative and UNRATE rises concurrently
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising HOUST and RSAFS jointly indicate rebuilding housing demand and household spending, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-shock periods with simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS strengthening do not outperform the full sample on 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal loses significance after controlling for either proxy alone.
